title: abutilon fraseri var. halophilum
intro: Abutilon fraseri var. halophilum, also known as the salt-marsh mallow, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the Malvaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant is rich in mucilages, which provide health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Abutilon fraseri var. halophilum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
